---
title: "Session timetable"
order: 1
comments: false
---

This is the **nowcasting** course, taught in the first half of the SISMID week.
It is paired with the companion [forecasting & evaluation course](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid-forecasting/).
The schedule below is a proposed plan, with timings based on how long the sessions have taken to run in previous courses.

# Day 1 (Monday): Epidemiological delays

## Session 0: Introduction and course overview

Monday: 09.00-09.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-the-course-and-the-instructors): the course and the instructors (10 mins)
-   [Motivating the course](../sessions/slides/from-line-list-to-decisions): from an epidemiological line list to informing decisions in real-time (20 mins)

## Getting set up

Monday: 09.30-10.15

-   [Getting set up for the course](../getting-started): installing R, the `nfidd.nowcasting` package and cmdstan, and checking everyone's environment is working (45 mins)

## Session 1: Delay distributions

Monday: 10.15-10.30 and 11.00-11.45

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-epidemiological-delays): epidemiological delays and how to represent them with probability distributions (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/delay-distributions): simulate and estimate epidemiological delays (45 mins)
-   Wrap up (5 mins)

## Session 2: Biases in delay distributions

Monday: 11.45-12.30 and 13.30-14.15

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-biases-in-epidemiological-delays): biases in delay distributions (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/biases-in-delay-distributions):
    -   interval censoring - estimating delays from discrete dates (35 mins)
    -   right truncation - estimating delays in real-time with incomplete data (35 mins)
-   Wrap up (10 mins)

## Session 3: Using delay distributions to model the data generating process of an epidemic

Monday: 14.45-15.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/convolutions): using delay distributions to model the data generating process of an epidemic (15 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/using-delay-distributions-to-model-the-data-generating-process-of-an-epidemic): implementing a convolution model and identifying potential problems (30 mins)

# Day 2 (Tuesday): Estimating transmission and nowcasting

Tuesday: 09.00-09.15

-   Day 1 review (15 mins)

## Session 4: $R_t$ estimation and the renewal equation

Tuesday: 09.15-10.45

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-reproduction-number): the time-varying reproduction number (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/R-estimation-and-the-renewal-equation): using the renewal equation to estimate R (75 mins)
-   Wrap up (5 mins)

## Session 5: Nowcasting concepts

Tuesday: 11.15-12.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-nowcasting): nowcasting as a right-truncation problem (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/nowcasting): simulating and basic nowcasting (60 mins)
-   Wrap up (5 mins)

## Session 6: Nowcasting with an unknown reporting delay

Tuesday: 13.30-15.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-joint-estimation-of-nowcasting-and-reporting-delays): joint estimation of delays and nowcasts (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/joint-nowcasting):
    -   joint estimation of delays and nowcasts
    -   joint estimation of delays, nowcasts and reproduction numbers (70 mins)
-   Wrap up (10 mins)

## Session 7: Jointly fitting multiple data streams

Tuesday: 15.30-17.00

-   [Practice](../sessions/multiple-data-streams):
    -   combining cases, deaths and a wastewater signal in one joint renewal model (75 mins)
    -   exploring what happens when streams conflict (10 mins)
-   Wrap up (5 mins)

# Day 3 (Wednesday): Real-world methods and bridging to forecasting

Wednesday: 09.00-09.15

-   Day 2 review (15 mins)

## Session 8: Methods in the real world

Wednesday: 09.15-10.15

-   [Methods in the real world](../sessions/methods-in-the-real-world): presentations and discussion on real-world uses of nowcasts and reproduction number estimates (60 mins)

## Session 9: Combining nowcasting and forecasting

Wednesday: 10.45-11.45

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/forecasting-nowcasting): why combine nowcasting and forecasting? (10 mins)
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecasting-nowcasting): connecting nowcasting and forecasting (45 mins)
-   Wrap up (5 mins)

## Session 10: Summary and discussion

Wednesday: 11.45-12.15

-   [Summary](../sessions/slides/closing) of the course (10 mins)
-   Final discussion and [further reading](../sessions/end-of-course-summary-and-discussion) (20 mins)
